general_info.definition: Nitrogen and phosphorous doped carbon quantum dots for sensing dopamine (DA) and temperature in aqueous medium

method.conditions: 150°C in 1min using 850W microwave irradiations. Reaction was proceeded at this temperature for 30min through controlling the duration of microwave ir radiation (700W)

structural.morphology: Narrow size distribution with spherical shape

applications.performance: Linear range: 10-500 μM (R²=0.994), detection limit: 0.021 μM
applications.comparative_advantage: good quantum yield of 44% in a simple as well as greener way, tremendous optical performance, notable fluorescence, excellent water solubility and thermosensitivity.
